You assist users with a wide range of tasks including answering questions, writing and editing code, analyzing information, creative work, and executing actions via your tools. You communicate clearly, admit uncertainty when appropriate, and prioritize being genuinely useful over being verbose unless otherwise directed below. Be targeted and efficient in your exploration and investigations.

When the user references something from a past conversation or you suspect relevant cross-session context exists, use session_search to recall it before asking them to repeat themselves. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

## Skills (mandatory)
Before replying, scan the skills below. If one clearly matches your task, load it with skill_view(name) and follow its instructions. If a skill has issues, fix it with skill_manage(action='patch').
After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. If a skill you loaded was missing steps, had wrong commands, or needed pitfalls you discovered, update it before finishing.

function.name: browser_back
function.description: Navigate back to the previous page in browser history. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

function.name: browser_click
function.description: Click on an element identified by its ref ID from the snapshot (e.g., '@e5'). The ref IDs are shown in square brackets in the snapshot output. Requires browser_navigate and browser_snapshot to be called first.

ref.description: The element reference from the snapshot (e.g., '@e5', '@e12')

function.name: browser_console
function.description: Get browser console output and JavaScript errors from the current page. Returns console.log/warn/error/info messages and uncaught JS exceptions. Use this to detect silent JavaScript errors, failed API calls, and application warnings. Requires browser_navigate to be called first. When 'expression' is provided, evaluates JavaScript in the page context and returns the result — use this for DOM inspection, reading page state, or extracting data programmatically.

clear.description: If true, clear the message buffers after reading

expression.description: JavaScript expression to evaluate in the page context. Runs in the browser like DevTools console — full access to DOM, window, document. Return values are serialized to JSON. Example: 'document.title' or 'document.querySelectorAll("a").length'

function.name: browser_get_images
function.description: Get a list of all images on the current page with their URLs and alt text. Useful for finding images to analyze with the vision tool. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

function.name: browser_navigate
function.description: Navigate to a URL in the browser. Initializes the session and loads the page. Must be called before other browser tools. Use browser tools when you need to interact with a page (click, fill forms, dynamic content). Returns a compact page snapshot with interactive elements and ref IDs — no need to call browser_snapshot separately after navigating.

function.name: browser_press
function.description: Press a keyboard key. Useful for submitting forms (Enter), navigating (Tab), or keyboard shortcuts. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

function.name: browser_scroll
function.description: Scroll the page in a direction. Use this to reveal more content that may be below or above the current viewport. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

function.name: browser_snapshot
function.description: Get a text-based snapshot of the current page's accessibility tree. Returns interactive elements with ref IDs (like @e1, @e2) for browser_click and browser_type. full=false (default): compact view with interactive elements. full=true: complete page content. Snapshots over 8000 chars are truncated or LLM-summarized. Requires browser_navigate first. Note: browser_navigate already returns a compact snapshot — use this to refresh after interactions that change the page, or with full=true for complete content.

full.description: If true, returns complete page content. If false (default), returns compact view with interactive elements only.

function.name: browser_type
function.description: Type text into an input field identified by its ref ID. Clears the field first, then types the new text. Requires browser_navigate and browser_snapshot to be called first.

ref.description: The element reference from the snapshot (e.g., '@e3')

text.description: The text to type into the field

function.name: browser_vision
function.description: Take a screenshot of the current page and analyze it with vision AI. Use this when you need to visually understand what's on the page - especially useful for CAPTCHAs, visual verification challenges, complex layouts, or when the text snapshot doesn't capture important visual information. Returns both the AI analysis and a screenshot_path that you can share with the user by including MEDIA:<screenshot_path> in your response. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

question.description: What you want to know about the page visually. Be specific about what you're looking for.

annotate.description: If true, overlay numbered [N] labels on interactive elements. Each [N] maps to ref @eN for subsequent browser commands. Useful for QA and spatial reasoning about page layout.

function.name: cronjob
function.description:
Manage scheduled cron jobs with a single compressed tool.

Use action='create' to schedule a new job from a prompt or one or more skills.
Use action='list' to inspect jobs.
Use action='update', 'pause', 'resume', 'remove', or 'run' to manage an existing job.

Jobs run in a fresh session with no current-chat context, so prompts must be self-contained.
If skills are provided on create, the future cron run loads those skills in order, then follows the prompt as the task instruction.
On update, passing skills=[] clears attached skills.

NOTE: The agent's final response is auto-delivered to the target. Put the primary
user-facing content in the final response. Cron jobs run autonomously with no user
present — they cannot ask questions or request clarification.

Important safety rule: cron-run sessions should not recursively schedule more cron jobs.

action.description: One of: create, list, update, pause, resume, remove, run

prompt.description: For create: the full self-contained prompt. If skills are also provided, this becomes the task instruction paired with those skills.

repeat.description: Optional repeat count. Omit for defaults (once for one-shot, forever for recurring).

deliver.description: Delivery target: origin, local, telegram, discord, slack, whatsapp, signal, matrix, mattermost, homeassistant, dingtalk, feishu, wecom, email, sms, or platform:chat_id or platform:chat_id:thread_id for Telegram topics. Examples: 'origin', 'local', 'telegram', 'telegram:-1001234567890:17585', 'discord:#engineering'

skills.description: Optional ordered list of skill names to load before executing the cron prompt. On update, pass an empty array to clear attached skills.

model.description: Optional per-job model override. If provider is omitted, the current main provider is pinned at creation time so the job stays stable.

provider.description: Provider name (e.g. 'openrouter', 'anthropic'). Omit to use and pin the current provider.

script.description: Optional path to a Python script that runs before each cron job execution. Its stdout is injected into the prompt as context. Use for data collection and change detection. Relative paths resolve under ~/.hermes/scripts/. On update, pass empty string to clear.

function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: session_search
function.description:
Search your long-term memory of past conversations, or browse recent sessions. This is your recall -- every past session is searchable, and this tool summarizes what happened.

TWO MODES:
1. Recent sessions (no query): Call with no arguments to see what was worked on recently. Returns titles, previews, and timestamps. Zero LLM cost, instant. Start here when the user asks what were we working on or what did we do recently.
2. Keyword search (with query): Search for specific topics across all past sessions. Returns LLM-generated summaries of matching sessions.

USE THIS PROACTIVELY when:
- The user says 'we did this before', 'remember when', 'last time', 'as I mentioned'
- The user asks about a topic you worked on before but don't have in current context
- The user references a project, person, or concept that seems familiar but isn't in memory
- You want to check if you've solved a similar problem before
- The user asks 'what did we do about X?' or 'how did we fix Y?'

Don't hesitate to search when it is actually cross-session -- it's fast and cheap. Better to search and confirm than to guess or ask the user to repeat themselves.

Search syntax: keywords joined with OR for broad recall (elevenlabs OR baseten OR funding), phrases for exact match ("docker networking"), boolean (python NOT java), prefix (deploy*). IMPORTANT: Use OR between keywords for best results — FTS5 defaults to AND which misses sessions that only mention some terms. If a broad OR query returns nothing, try individual keyword searches in parallel. Returns summaries of the top matching sessions.

query.description: Search query — keywords, phrases, or boolean expressions to find in past sessions. Omit this parameter entirely to browse recent sessions instead (returns titles, previews, timestamps with no LLM cost).

role_filter.description: Optional: only search messages from specific roles (comma-separated). E.g. 'user,assistant' to skip tool outputs.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills)

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.
Important: cloud sandboxes may be cleaned up, idled out, or recreated between turns. Persistent filesystem means files can resume later; it does NOT guarantee a continuously running machine or surviving background processes. Use terminal sandboxes for task work, not durable hosting.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

check_interval.description: Seconds between automatic status checks for background processes (gateway/messaging only, minimum 30). When set, I'll proactively report progress.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified when the process finishes — no polling needed. Use this for tasks that take a while (tests, builds, deployments) so you can keep working on other things in the meantime.

function.name: text_to_speech
function.description: Convert text to speech audio. Returns a MEDIA: path that the platform delivers as a voice message. On Telegram it plays as a voice bubble, on Discord/WhatsApp as an audio attachment. In CLI mode, saves to ~/voice-memos/. Voice and provider are user-configured, not model-selected.

text.description: The text to convert to speech. Keep under 4000 characters.

output_path.description: Optional custom file path to save the audio. Defaults to ~/.hermes/audio_cache/<timestamp>.mp3

function.name: todo
function.description:
Manage your task list for the current session. Use for complex tasks with 3+ steps or when the user provides multiple tasks. Call with no parameters to read the current list.

Writing:
- Provide 'todos' array to create/update items
- merge=false (default): replace the entire list with a fresh plan
- merge=true: update existing items by id, add any new ones

Each item: {id: string, content: string, status: pending|in_progress|completed|cancelled}
List order is priority. Only ONE item in_progress at a time.
Mark items completed immediately when done. If something fails, cancel it and add a revised item.

Always returns the full current list.

todos.description: Task items to write. Omit to read current list.

merge.description: true: update existing items by id, add new ones. false (default): replace the entire list.

function.name: vision_analyze
function.description: Analyze images using AI vision. Provides a comprehensive description and answers a specific question about the image content.

image_url.description: Image URL (http/https) or local file path to analyze.

question.description: Your specific question or request about the image to resolve. The AI will automatically provide a complete image description AND answer your specific question.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits.

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

The command must exist on `PATH` and launch successfully outside ClawTeam. 517|2. The agent must be able to run inside a specific working directory or git worktree. 518|3.     12|    """Validate a logical identifier used in filesystem-backed state."""
    13|    if value == "" and allow_empty:
    14|        return value

    17|    if not _IDENTIFIER_RE.fullmatch(value):

    21|    return value

    24|def ensure_within_root(root: Path, *parts: str) -> Path:
    25|    """Join *parts* under *root* and reject escapes outside the root."""
    26|    base = root.resolve()
    27|    candidate = root.joinpath(*parts)
    28|    resolved = candidate.resolve(strict=False)
    29|    try:
    30|        resolved.relative_to(base)
    31|    except ValueError as exc:
    32|        raise ValueError("Resolved path escapes the configured data directory") from exc

    24|def _tasks_root(team_name: str) -> Path:

    37|def _tasks_lock_path(team_name: str) -> Path:
    38|    return _tasks_root(team_name) / ".tasks.lock"

    44|
    45|class FileTaskStore(BaseTaskStore):
    46|    """Task store backed by the local filesystem.
    47|
    48|    Each task is stored as a separate JSON file:
    49|    ``{data_dir}/tasks/{team}/task-{id}.json``
    50|
    51|    Concurrent access is serialised with an OS-specific advisory lock.
    52|    """
    53|
    54|    @contextmanager
    55|    def _write_lock(self):
    56|        lock_path = _tasks_lock_path(self.team_name)
    57|        lock_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    58|        with lock_path.open("a+", encoding="utf-8") as lock_file:
    59|            if sys.platform == "win32":
    60|                pos = lock_file.tell()
    61|                lock_file.seek(0)
    62|                msvcrt.locking(lock_file.fileno(), msvcrt.LK_LOCK, 1)
    63|                lock_file.seek(pos)
    64|            else:
    65|                fcntl.flock(lock_file.fileno(), fcntl.LOCK_EX)
    66|            try:
    67|                yield
    68|            finally:
    69|                if sys.platform == "win32":
    70|                    pos = lock_file.tell()
    71|                    lock_file.seek(0)
    72|                    msvcrt.locking(lock_file.fileno(), msvcrt.LK_UNLCK, 1)
    73|                    lock_file.seek(pos)
    74|                else:
    75|                    fcntl.flock(lock_file.fileno(), fcntl.LOCK_UN)

    77|    def create(

   111|    def get(self, task_id: str) -> TaskItem | None:

   124|    def update(

   128|        owner: str | None = None,
   129|        subject: str | None = None,
   130|        description: str | None = None,
   131|        priority: TaskPriority | None = None,
   132|        add_blocks: list[str] | None = None,
   133|        add_blocked_by: list[str] | None = None,
   134|        metadata: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
   135|        caller: str = "",
   136|        force: bool = False,
   137|    ) -> TaskItem | None:
   138|        with self._write_lock():
   139|            task = self._get_unlocked(task_id)
   140|            if not task:
   141|                return None
   142|            _old_status = task.status.value
   143|
   144|            if status == TaskStatus.in_progress:
   145|                self._acquire_lock(task, caller, force)
   146|                if not task.started_at:
   147|                    task.started_at = _now_iso()
   148|                if owner is None and caller and not task.owner:
   149|                    task.owner = caller
   150|
   151|            if status in (TaskStatus.completed, TaskStatus.pending):
   152|                task.locked_by = ""
   153|                task.locked_at = ""
   154|
   155|            # duration tracking
   156|            if status == TaskStatus.completed and task.started_at:
   157|                try:
   158|                    start = datetime.fromisoformat(task.started_at)
   159|                    duration_secs = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - start).total_seconds()
   160|                    task.metadata["duration_seconds"] = round(duration_secs, 2)
   161|                except (ValueError, TypeError):
   162|                    pass
   163|
   164|            if status is not None:
   165|                task.status = status
   166|            if owner is not None:
   167|                task.owner = owner
   168|            if subject is not None:
   169|                task.subject = subject
   170|            if description is not None:
   171|                task.description = description
   172|            if priority is not None:
   173|                task.priority = priority
   174|            if add_blocks:
   175|                for b in add_blocks:
   176|                    if b not in task.blocks:
   177|                        task.blocks.append(b)
   178|            if add_blocked_by:
   179|                proposed_blocked_by = list(task.blocked_by)
   180|                for b in add_blocked_by:
   181|                    if b not in proposed_blocked_by:
   182|                        proposed_blocked_by.append(b)

   184|                task.blocked_by = proposed_blocked_by

   186|                    task.status = TaskStatus.blocked

   199|            from clawteam.events.types import AfterTaskUpdate, TaskCompleted

   201|            bus.emit_async(AfterTaskUpdate(

   203|                old_status=_old_status, new_status=task.status.value,
   204|                owner=task.owner,
   205|            ))

   207|                bus.emit_async(TaskCompleted(
   208|                    team_name=self.team_name, task_id=task_id,
   209|                    owner=task.owner,
   210|                    duration_seconds=task.metadata.get("duration_seconds", 0.0),
   211|                ))
   212|        except Exception:

   216|    def _acquire_lock(self, task: TaskItem, caller: str, force: bool) -> None:
   217|        if task.locked_by and task.locked_by != caller and not force:
   218|            from clawteam.spawn.registry import is_agent_alive
   219|            alive = is_agent_alive(self.team_name, task.locked_by)
   220|            if alive is not False:
   221|                raise TaskLockError(
   222|                    f"Task '{task.id}' is locked by '{task.locked_by}' "
   223|                    f"(since {task.locked_at}). Use --force to override."
   224|                )
   225|        task.locked_by = caller or ""
   226|        task.locked_at = _now_iso() if caller else ""
   227|
   228|    def release_stale_locks(self) -> list[str]:
   229|        from clawteam.spawn.registry import is_agent_alive
   230|
   231|        released = []
   232|        with self._write_lock():
   233|            for task in self._list_tasks_unlocked():
   234|                if not task.locked_by:
   235|                    continue
   236|                alive = is_agent_alive(self.team_name, task.locked_by)
   237|                if alive is False:
   238|                    task.locked_by = ""
   239|                    task.locked_at = ""
   240|                    task.updated_at = _now_iso()
   241|                    self._save_unlocked(task)
   242|                    released.append(task.id)
   243|        return released
   244|
   245|    def list_tasks(
   246|        self,
   247|        status: TaskStatus | None = None,

   253|            status=status,

   255|            priority=priority,
   256|            sort_by_priority=sort_by_priority,
   257|        )

   259|    def _list_tasks_unlocked(
   260|        self,
   261|        status: TaskStatus | None = None,
   262|        owner: str | None = None,
   263|        priority: TaskPriority | None = None,
   264|        sort_by_priority: bool = False,
   265|    ) -> list[TaskItem]:
   266|        root = _tasks_root(self.team_name)
   267|        tasks = []
   268|        for f in sorted(root.glob("task-*.json")):
   269|            try:
   270|                data = json.loads(f.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))

   272|                if status and task.status != status:
   273|                    continue
   274|                if owner and task.owner != owner:
   275|                    continue
   276|                if priority and task.priority != priority:
   277|                    continue
   278|                tasks.append(task)
   279|            except Exception:
   280|                continue
   281|        if sort_by_priority:
   282|            priority_order = {
   283|                TaskPriority.urgent: 0,
   284|                TaskPriority.high: 1,
   285|                TaskPriority.medium: 2,
   286|                TaskPriority.low: 3,
   287|            }
   288|            tasks.sort(key=lambda task: (priority_order.get(task.priority, 2), task.created_at, task.id))
   289|        return tasks

   291|    def _validate_blocked_by_unlocked(self, task_id: str, blocked_by: list[str]) -> None:
   292|        if task_id in blocked_by:
   293|            raise ValueError(f"Task '{task_id}' cannot be blocked by itself")

   295|        graph: dict[str, list[str]] = {
   296|            task.id: list(task.blocked_by)
   297|            for task in self._list_tasks_unlocked()
   298|        }
   299|        graph[task_id] = list(blocked_by)
   300|
   301|        visiting: set[str] = set()
   302|        visited: set[str] = set()
   303|
   304|        def _visit(node: str) -> bool:
   305|            if node in visiting:
   306|                return True
   307|            if node in visited:
   308|                return False
   309|            visiting.add(node)

   311|                if dep in graph and _visit(dep):

   316|
   317|        for node in graph:
   318|            if _visit(node):

   347|                    task.updated_at = _now_iso()

    40|
    41|
    42|class HarnessConductor:
    43|    """Drives the harness through phases automatically.
    44|
    45|    Runs as a foreground polling loop (like InboxWatcher).
    46|    Ctrl+C to stop gracefully.
    47|    """
    48|
    49|    def __init__(
    50|        self,
    51|        orchestrator: HarnessOrchestrator,
    52|        spawn_strategy: SpawnStrategy | None = None,
    53|        respawn_strategy: RespawnStrategy | None = None,
    54|        health_strategy: HealthStrategy | None = None,
    55|        exit_notifier: ExitNotifier | None = None,
    56|        poll_interval: float = 5.0,
    57|        health_interval: float = 30.0,
    58|    ) -> None:
    59|        self._orch = orchestrator
    60|        self._spawn = spawn_strategy or PhaseRoleSpawner(
    61|            cli=orchestrator.cli,
    62|        )
    63|        self._respawn = respawn_strategy or NoRespawn()
    64|        self._health = health_strategy or RegistryHealthCheck()
    65|        self._exit_notifier = exit_notifier or FileExitJournal(
    66|            orchestrator.team_name, orchestrator.state.harness_id,
    67|        )
    68|        self._poll_interval = poll_interval
    69|        self._health_interval = health_interval
    70|        self._running = False
    71|        self._last_health_check = 0.0
    72|
    73|    def build_context(self) -> HarnessContext:
    74|        """Build a HarnessContext for plugins."""
    75|        from clawteam.events.global_bus import get_event_bus
    76|        return HarnessContext(
    77|            bus=get_event_bus(),
    78|            team_name=self._orch.team_name,
    79|            spawner=self._spawn,
    80|            artifacts=self._orch.artifacts,
    81|        )
    82|
    83|    def run(self) -> None:
    84|        """Start the conductor loop. Blocks until harness completes or Ctrl+C."""
    85|        self._running = True
    86|
    87|        def _handle_signal(signum, frame):
    88|            self._running = False
    89|            print("\n[conductor] Stopping...", file=sys.stderr)
    90|
    91|        signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, _handle_signal)
    92|        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handle_signal)
    93|
    94|        print(f"[conductor] Starting harness {self._orch.state.harness_id}", file=sys.stderr)
    95|        print(f"[conductor] Phase: {self._orch.state.current_phase}", file=sys.stderr)
    96|
    97|        # Initial spawn for current phase
    98|        spawned = self._spawn.spawn_for_phase(
    99|            self._orch.state.current_phase, self._orch,
   100|        )
   101|        if spawned:
   102|            print(f"[conductor] Spawned: {', '.join(spawned)}", file=sys.stderr)
   103|
   104|        self._last_health_check = time.monotonic()
   105|
   106|        while self._running:
   107|            # 1. Read exit journal for cross-process exit notifications
   108|            exits = self._exit_notifier.read_new()
   109|            for exit_info in exits:
   110|                agent = exit_info.get("agent_name", "")
   111|                print(f"[conductor] Agent exited: {agent}", file=sys.stderr)
   112|                self._respawn.on_agent_exit(
   113|                    agent_name=agent,
   114|                    team_name=self._orch.team_name,
   115|                    exit_info=exit_info,
   116|                    spawn_strategy=self._spawn,
   117|                )
   118|
   119|            # 2. Try to advance phase
   120|            new_phase = self._orch.advance()
   121|            if new_phase:
   122|                print(f"[conductor] Advanced to phase: {new_phase}", file=sys.stderr)
   123|                if new_phase == "execute":
   124|                    # Spawn executors first so task assignment can target real agent names.
   125|                    spawned = self._spawn.spawn_for_phase(new_phase, self._orch)
   126|                    if spawned:
   127|                        print(f"[conductor] Spawned: {', '.join(spawned)}", file=sys.stderr)
   128|                    self._prepare_execute(executor_names=spawned)
   129|                else:
   130|                    spawned = self._spawn.spawn_for_phase(new_phase, self._orch)
   131|                    if spawned:
   132|                        print(f"[conductor] Spawned: {', '.join(spawned)}", file=sys.stderr)
   133|
   134|            # 3. Check if we're at the final phase
   135|            phases = self._orch.state.phases
   136|            if self._orch.state.current_phase == phases[-1]:
   137|                print(f"[conductor] Harness complete (phase: {phases[-1]})", file=sys.stderr)
   138|                self._running = False
   139|                break
   140|
   141|            # 4. Periodic health check
   142|            now = time.monotonic()
   143|            if now - self._last_health_check > self._health_interval:
   144|                issues = self._health.check(self._orch.team_name)
   145|                for issue in issues:
   146|                    print(f"[conductor] Health issue: {issue}", file=sys.stderr)
   147|                self._last_health_check = now
   148|
   149|            time.sleep(self._poll_interval)
   150|
   151|        print("[conductor] Stopped.", file=sys.stderr)
   152|
   153|    def _prepare_execute(self, executor_names: list[str] | None = None) -> None:
   154|        """Prepare the execute phase: load contracts, create tasks."""
   155|        try:
   156|            from clawteam.harness.contract_executor import ContractExecutor
   157|            from clawteam.team.manager import TeamManager
   158|
   159|            available_executors = list(executor_names or [])
   160|            if not available_executors:
   161|                team = TeamManager.get_team(self._orch.team_name)
   162|                if team:
   163|                    available_executors = [
   164|                        member.name
   165|                        for member in team.members
   166|                        if member.agent_type == "executor"
   167|                    ]
   168|
   169|            executor = ContractExecutor(self._orch)
   170|            tasks = executor.create_tasks_from_contracts(agent_names=available_executors)
   171|            if tasks:
   172|                print(f"[conductor] Created {len(tasks)} task(s) from contracts", file=sys.stderr)
   173|        except Exception as e:
   174|            print(f"[conductor] Warning: failed to create tasks: {e}", file=sys.stderr)

     2|
     3|from __future__ import annotations
     4|
     5|import json
     6|import uuid
     7|from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
     8|from datetime import datetime, timezone
     9|from pathlib import Path
    10|from typing import Any
    11|
    12|from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
    13|
    14|
    15|def _now_iso() -> str:
    16|    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
    17|
    18|
    19|# Phase is an open str — not an Enum. Plugins/templates can define custom phases.
    20|Phase = str
    21|
    22|DISCUSS: Phase = "discuss"
    23|PLAN: Phase = "plan"
    24|EXECUTE: Phase = "execute"
    25|VERIFY: Phase = "verify"
    26|SHIP: Phase = "ship"
    27|
    28|DEFAULT_PHASES: list[Phase] = [DISCUSS, PLAN, EXECUTE, VERIFY, SHIP]
    29|
    30|DEFAULT_PHASE_ROLES: dict[str, str] = {
    31|    "discuss": "planner",
    32|    "plan": "planner",
    33|    "execute": "executor",
    34|    "verify": "evaluator",
    35|    "ship": "",
    36|}
    37|
    38|
    39|class PhaseState(BaseModel):
    40|    """Persisted state of a harness run."""
    41|
    42|    harness_id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: uuid.uuid4().hex[:12])
    43|    team_name: str = ""
    44|    current_phase: str = DISCUSS
    45|    phases: list[str] = Field(default_factory=lambda: list(DEFAULT_PHASES))
    46|    phase_roles: dict[str, str] = Field(default_factory=lambda: dict(DEFAULT_PHASE_ROLES))
    47|    phase_history: list[dict[str, Any]] = Field(default_factory=list)
    48|    artifacts: dict[str, str] = Field(default_factory=dict)
    49|    goal: str = ""
    50|    cli: str = "claude"
    51|    agent_count: int = 3
    52|    created_at: str = Field(default_factory=_now_iso)
    53|    updated_at: str = Field(default_factory=_now_iso)
    54|
    55|
    56|class PhaseGate(ABC):
    57|    """Gate that must pass before a phase can advance."""
    58|
    59|    @abstractmethod
    60|    def check(self, state: PhaseState) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    61|        """Return (passed, reason)."""
    62|
    63|
    64|class ArtifactRequiredGate(PhaseGate):
    65|    """Requires specific artifacts to exist."""
    66|
    67|    def __init__(self, artifact_names: list[str]):
    68|        self.artifact_names = artifact_names
    69|
    70|    def check(self, state: PhaseState) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    71|        missing = [n for n in self.artifact_names if n not in state.artifacts]
    72|        if missing:
    73|            return False, f"Missing artifacts: {', '.join(missing)}"
    74|        return True, ""
    75|
    76|
    77|class AllTasksCompleteGate(PhaseGate):
    78|    """Requires all team tasks to be completed."""
    79|
    80|    def check(self, state: PhaseState) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    81|        from clawteam.team.models import TaskStatus
    82|        from clawteam.team.tasks import TaskStore
    83|        store = TaskStore(state.team_name)
    84|        tasks = store.list_tasks()
    85|        incomplete = [t for t in tasks if t.status != TaskStatus.completed]
    86|        if incomplete:
    87|            return False, f"{len(incomplete)} task(s) not completed"
    88|        return True, ""
    89|
    90|
    91|class HumanApprovalGate(PhaseGate):
    92|    """Requires explicit human approval before advancing.
    93|
    94|    Approval is stored as an artifact: approval-{phase_name}.json
    95|    """
    96|
    97|    def __init__(self, phase_name: str):
    98|        self._artifact_name = f"approval-{phase_name}.json"
    99|
   100|    def check(self, state: PhaseState) -> tuple[bool, str]:
   101|        if self._artifact_name not in state.artifacts:
   102|            return False, f"Human approval required: oh harness approve {state.team_name}"
   103|        return True, ""
   104|
   105|
   106|class PhaseRunner:
   107|    """Manages phase transitions with gate checking."""
   108|
   109|    def __init__(self, state: PhaseState) -> None:
   110|        self.state = state
   111|        self._gates: dict[str, list[PhaseGate]] = {}
   112|
   113|    def register_gate(self, phase: str, gate: PhaseGate) -> None:
   114|        """Register a gate that must pass before *phase* can advance."""
   115|        self._gates.setdefault(phase, []).append(gate)
   116|
   117|    def can_advance(self) -> tuple[bool, str]:
   118|        """Check if the current phase can advance."""
   119|        gates = self._gates.get(self.state.current_phase, [])
   120|        for gate in gates:
   121|            ok, reason = gate.check(self.state)
   122|            if not ok:
   123|                return False, reason
   124|        return True, ""
   125|
   126|    def advance(self) -> str | None:
   127|        """Advance to the next phase. Returns new phase or None if blocked/finished."""
   128|        ok, reason = self.can_advance()
   129|        if not ok:
   130|            return None
   131|
   132|        phases = self.state.phases
   133|        idx = phases.index(self.state.current_phase)
   134|        if idx >= len(phases) - 1:
   135|            return None  # already at last phase
   136|
   137|        old_phase = self.state.current_phase
   138|        new_phase = phases[idx + 1]
   139|
   140|        self.state.phase_history.append({
   141|            "phase": old_phase,
   142|            "completed_at": _now_iso(),
   143|        })
   144|        self.state.current_phase = new_phase
   145|        self.state.updated_at = _now_iso()
   146|
   147|        # Emit PhaseTransition event
   148|        try:
   149|            from clawteam.events.global_bus import get_event_bus
   150|            from clawteam.events.types import PhaseTransition
   151|            get_event_bus().emit(PhaseTransition(
   152|                team_name=self.state.team_name,
   153|                from_phase=old_phase,
   154|                to_phase=new_phase,
   155|                artifacts=list(self.state.artifacts.keys()),
   156|            ))
   157|        except Exception:
   158|            pass
   159|
   160|        return new_phase
   161|
   162|    def rollback(self, to_phase: str) -> str | None:
   163|        """Rollback to a previous phase."""
   164|        phases = self.state.phases
   165|        if to_phase not in phases:
   166|            return None
   167|        target_idx = phases.index(to_phase)
   168|        current_idx = phases.index(self.state.current_phase)
   169|        if target_idx >= current_idx:
   170|            return None  # can't rollback forward
   171|        self.state.current_phase = to_phase
   172|        self.state.updated_at = _now_iso()
   173|        return to_phase
   174|
   175|    # ── Persistence ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
   176|
   177|    def save(self, base_dir: Path) -> Path:
   178|        """Save state to disk."""
   179|        harness_dir = base_dir / self.state.team_name / self.state.harness_id
   180|        harness_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   181|        state_path = harness_dir / "state.json"
   182|        state_path.write_text(
   183|            self.state.model_dump_json(indent=2), encoding="utf-8"
   184|        )
   185|        return state_path
   186|
   187|    @classmethod
   188|    def load(cls, state_path: Path) -> PhaseRunner:
   189|        """Load state from disk."""
   190|        data = json.loads(state_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   191|        state = PhaseState.model_validate(data)
   192|        return cls(state)

     2|
     3|from __future__ import annotations
     4|
     5|from pathlib import Path
     6|
     7|from clawteam.harness.artifacts import ArtifactStore
     8|from clawteam.harness.phases import (
     9|    PLAN,
    10|    VERIFY,
    11|    AllTasksCompleteGate,
    12|    ArtifactRequiredGate,

    17|from clawteam.harness.roles import DEFAULT_ROLES
    18|from clawteam.team.models import get_data_dir
    19|
    20|
    21|class HarnessOrchestrator:
    22|    """Orchestrates a plan-then-execute harness run."""
    23|
    24|    def __init__(
    25|        self,
    26|        team_name: str,
    27|        goal: str = "",
    28|        cli: str = "claude",
    29|        agent_count: int = 3,
    30|        phases: list[str] | None = None,
    31|        phase_roles: dict[str, str] | None = None,
    32|        human_gates: list[str] | None = None,
    33|    ) -> None:
    34|        self.team_name = team_name
    35|        self.goal = goal
    36|        self.cli = cli
    37|        self.agent_count = agent_count
    38|
    39|        self.state = PhaseState(
    40|            team_name=team_name,
    41|            goal=goal,
    42|            cli=cli,
    43|            agent_count=agent_count,
    44|        )
    45|        if phases:
    46|            self.state.phases = phases
    47|        if phase_roles:
    48|            self.state.phase_roles.update(phase_roles)
    49|
    50|        self.runner = PhaseRunner(self.state)
    51|        self.artifacts = ArtifactStore(
    52|            self._harness_dir(), team_name, self.state.harness_id,
    53|        )
    54|
    55|        # Default gates
    56|        self.runner.register_gate(PLAN, ArtifactRequiredGate(["spec.md"]))
    57|        self.runner.register_gate(VERIFY, AllTasksCompleteGate())
    58|
    59|        # Human approval gates
    60|        for phase in (human_gates or [PLAN]):
    61|            self.runner.register_gate(phase, HumanApprovalGate(phase))
    62|
    63|    def _harness_dir(self) -> Path:
    64|        return get_data_dir() / "harness"
    65|
    66|    # ── Core operations ───────────────────────────────────────────────
    67|
    68|    def start(self) -> str:
    69|        """Start a new harness run. Returns harness_id."""
    70|        self.runner.save(self._harness_dir())
    71|        return self.state.harness_id
    72|
    73|    def advance(self) -> str | None:
    74|        """Try to advance to the next phase."""
    75|        result = self.runner.advance()
    76|        if result:
    77|            self.runner.save(self._harness_dir())
    78|        return result
    79|
    80|    def status(self) -> dict:
    81|        """Return current status."""
    82|        can_advance, reason = self.runner.can_advance()
    83|        return {
    84|            "harness_id": self.state.harness_id,
    85|            "team": self.team_name,
    86|            "goal": self.state.goal,
    87|            "phase": self.state.current_phase,
    88|            "can_advance": can_advance,
    89|            "gate_reason": reason,
    90|            "artifacts": list(self.state.artifacts.keys()),
    91|            "history": self.state.phase_history,
    92|        }
    93|
    94|    def register_artifact(self, name: str, path: str) -> None:
    95|        """Register an artifact in the harness state."""
    96|        self.state.artifacts[name] = path
    97|        self.runner.save(self._harness_dir())
    98|
    99|    def abort(self) -> None:
   100|        """Abort the harness run."""
   101|        self.state.phase_history.append({
   102|            "phase": self.state.current_phase,
   103|            "aborted_at": _now_iso(),
   104|        })
   105|        self.runner.save(self._harness_dir())
   106|
   107|    # ── Role helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
   108|
   109|    def get_role_config(self, role: str):
   110|        """Get the default RoleConfig for a given role."""
   111|        return DEFAULT_ROLES.get(role)
   112|
   113|    def get_role_for_phase(self, phase: str) -> str:
   114|        """Get the role name for a given phase."""
   115|        return self.state.phase_roles.get(phase, "")
   116|
   117|    # ── Class methods for loading ─────────────────────────────────────
   118|
   119|    @classmethod
   120|    def load(cls, team_name: str, harness_id: str) -> HarnessOrchestrator | None:
   121|        """Load an existing harness run."""
   122|        base = get_data_dir() / "harness"
   123|        state_path = base / team_name / harness_id / "state.json"
   124|        if not state_path.is_file():
   125|            return None
   126|        runner = PhaseRunner.load(state_path)
   127|        orch = cls.__new__(cls)
   128|        orch.team_name = team_name
   129|        orch.goal = runner.state.goal
   130|        orch.cli = runner.state.cli
   131|        orch.agent_count = runner.state.agent_count
   132|        orch.state = runner.state
   133|        orch.runner = runner
   134|        orch.artifacts = ArtifactStore(base, team_name, harness_id)
   135|        return orch
   136|
   137|    @classmethod
   138|    def find_latest(cls, team_name: str) -> HarnessOrchestrator | None:
   139|        """Find the most recent harness run for a team."""
   140|        base = get_data_dir() / "harness" / team_name
   141|        if not base.is_dir():
   142|            return None
   143|        runs = sorted(base.iterdir(), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
   144|        for run_dir in runs:
   145|            state_path = run_dir / "state.json"
   146|            if state_path.is_file():
   147|                return cls.load(team_name, run_dir.name)
   148|        return None
   149|

   152|    from datetime import datetime, timezone
   153|    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()

   295|        """Best-effort runtime injection into an existing tmux agent pane."""
   296|        if not shutil.which("tmux"):
   297|            return False, "tmux not installed"
   298|
   299|        target = f"{self.session_name(team)}:{agent_name}"
   300|        probe = subprocess.run(
   301|            ["tmux", "list-panes", "-t", target, "-F", "#{pane_id}"],
   302|            capture_output=True,
   303|            text=True,
   304|        )
   305|        if probe.returncode != 0 or not probe.stdout.strip():
   306|            return False, f"tmux target '{target}' not found"
   307|

   311|                agent_name,

   313|            )

   317|        return True, f"Injected runtime notification into {target}"

   321|        return f"clawteam-{team_name}"

   323|    @staticmethod
   324|    def tile_panes(team_name: str) -> str:
   325|        """Merge all windows into one tiled view. Does NOT attach.
   326|
   327|        Returns status message or error.
   328|        """
   329|        session = TmuxBackend.session_name(team_name)
   330|
   331|        check = subprocess.run(
   332|            ["tmux", "has-session", "-t", session],
   333|            stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   334|            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   335|        )
   336|        if check.returncode != 0:
   337|            return f"Error: tmux session '{session}' not found. No agents spawned for team '{team_name}'?"
   338|
   339|        # Count current panes in window 0
   340|        pane_count = subprocess.run(
   341|            ["tmux", "list-panes", "-t", f"{session}:0"],
   342|            capture_output=True, text=True,
   343|        )
   344|        num_panes = len(pane_count.stdout.strip().splitlines()) if pane_count.returncode == 0 else 0
   345|
   346|        # Get windows
   347|        result = subprocess.run(
   348|            ["tmux", "list-windows", "-t", session, "-F", "#{window_index}"],
   349|            capture_output=True, text=True,
   350|        )
   351|        if result.returncode != 0:
   352|            return f"Error: failed to list windows: {result.stderr.strip()}"
   353|
   354|        windows = result.stdout.strip().splitlines()
   355|
   356|        # If already tiled (1 window, multiple panes), skip merge
   357|        if len(windows) <= 1 and num_panes > 1:

   359|

   366|                )
   367|            subprocess.run(

   369|                stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
   370|            )
   371|
   372|        # Recount
   373|        pane_count = subprocess.run(
   374|            ["tmux", "list-panes", "-t", f"{session}:0"],
   375|            capture_output=True, text=True,
   376|        )
   377|        final_panes = len(pane_count.stdout.strip().splitlines()) if pane_count.returncode == 0 else 0
   378|        return f"Tiled {final_panes} panes in {session}"

{"content": "     1|"""Thin runtime router for tmux live injection."""
     2|
     3|from __future__ import annotations
     4|
     5|import json
     6|from datetime import datetime
     7|
     8|from clawteam.spawn.tmux_backend import TmuxBackend
     9|from clawteam.team.models import MessageType, TeamMessage
    10|from clawteam.team.routing_policy import DefaultRoutingPolicy, RouteDecision, RuntimeEnvelope
    11|
    12|
    13|class RuntimeRouter:
    14|    """Normalize inbox messages, ask policy for a decision, then dispatch."""
    15|
    16|    def __init__(
    17|        self,
    18|        team_name: str,
    19|        agent_name: str,
    20|        backend: TmuxBackend | None = None,
    21|        policy: DefaultRoutingPolicy | None = None,
    22|        session_agent_name: str | None = None,
    23|    ):
    24|        self.team_name = team_name
    25|        self.inbox_agent_name = agent_name
    26|        self.agent_name = session_agent_name or agent_name
    27|        self.backend = backend or TmuxBackend()
    28|        self.policy = policy or DefaultRoutingPolicy(team_name=team_name)
    29|
    30|    def normalize_message(self, message: TeamMessage) -> RuntimeEnvelope:
    31|        source = message.from_agent or "system"
    32|        # Route to the live tmux pane name when the message does not carry an explicit target.
    33|        target = message.to or self.agent_name
    34|        channel = "team" if message.type == MessageType.broadcast else "direct"
    35|        priority = self._priority_for_message(message)
    36|        evidence = []
    37|        if message.summary:
    38|            evidence.append(f"summary: {message.summary}")
    39|        if message.plan_file:
    40|            evidence.append(f"planFile: {message.plan_file}")
    41|        if message.status:
    42|            evidence.append(f"status: {message.status}")
    43|        if message.last_task:
    44|            evidence.append(f"lastTask: {message.last_task}")
    45|        if message.reason:
    46|            evidence.append(f"reason: {message.reason}")
    47|        if message.feedback:
    48|            evidence.append(f"feedback: {message.feedback}")
    49|        if message.request_id:
    50|            evidence.append(f"requestId: {message.request_id}")
    51|
    52|        summary = (message.content or "").strip() or f"{message.type.value} from {source}"
    53|        payload = json.loads(message.model_dump_json(by_alias=True, exclude_none=True))
    54|
    55|        return RuntimeEnvelope(
    56|            source=source,
    57|            target=target,
    58|            channel=channel,
    59|            priority=priority,
    60|            message_type=message.type.value,
    61|            summary=summary,
    62|            evidence=evidence,
    63|            recommended_next_action=self._recommended_next_action(message),
    64|            payload=payload,
    65|            dedupe_key=message.request_id or f"{source}:{target}:{message.type.value}:{message.timestamp}",
    66|            created_at=message.timestamp,
    67|        )
    68|
    69|    def route_message(
    70|        self,
    71|        message: TeamMessage,
    72|        *,
    73|        now: datetime | str | None = None,
    74|    ) -> RouteDecision:
    75|        envelope = self.normalize_message(message)
    76|        decision = self.policy.decide(envelope, now=now)
    77|        self.dispatch(decision, now=now)
    78|        return decision
    79|
    80|    def flush_due(self, *, now: datetime | str | None = None) -> list[RouteDecision]:
    81|        decisions = self.policy.flush_due(target_agent=self.agent_name, now=now)
    82|        for decision in decisions:
    83|            self.dispatch(decision, now=now)
    84|        return decisions
    85|
    86|    def dispatch(self, decision: RouteDecision, *, now: datetime | str | None = None) -> bool:
    87|        if decision.action != "inject" or not decision.envelope.requires_injection:
    88|            return False
    89|
    90|        if not hasattr(self.backend, "inject_runtime_message"):
    91|            self.policy.record_dispatch_result(
    92|                decision,
    93|                success=False,
    94|                now=now,
    95|                error="backend does not support runtime injection",
    96|            )
    97|            return False
    98|
    99|        ok, reason = self.backend.inject_runtime_message(
   100|            self.team_name,
   101|            decision.envelope.target,
   102|            decision.envelope,
   103|        )
   104|        self.policy.record_dispatch_result(
   105|            decision,
   106|            success=ok,
   107|            now=now,
   108|            error="" if ok else reason,
   109|        )
   110|        return ok
   111|
   112|    @staticmethod
   113|    def _priority_for_message(message: TeamMessage) -> str:
   114|        if message.type in {MessageType.shutdown_request, MessageType.shutdown_approved, MessageType.shutdown_rejected}:
   115|            return "high"
   116|        if message.type in {MessageType.idle, MessageType.plan_approval_request, MessageType.plan_rejected}:
   117|            return "high"
   118|        return "medium"
   119|
   120|    @staticmethod
   121|    def _recommended_next_action(message: TeamMessage) -> str | None:
   122|        if message.type == MessageType.plan_approval_request:
   123|            return "Review the plan and respond with an approval decision."
   124|        if message.type == MessageType.idle and message.last_task:
   125|            return f"Check blocker status for {message.last_task}."
   126|        return None

    31|        while IFS= read -r line || [ -n "$line" ]; do

    33|            [[ -z "$line" || "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]] && continue

    35|            export "$line" 2>/dev/null || true

    71|# 3. 执行任务 (禁用缓存，强制实时请求)

    87|echo "  OPENAI_API_KEY: ${OPENAI_API_KEY:+已设置(${#OPENAI_API_KEY}字符)}" >> "$LOG_FILE"

    92|RESULT=$(openclaw agent --local $AGENT_CMD_ARGS \

     3|from __future__ import annotations
     4|
     5|
     6|def build_harness_system_prompt(team: str, agent_name: str) -> str:
     7|    """Build a system prompt that gives an agent harness capabilities.
     8|
     9|    This is injected via --append-system-prompt when using `clawteam run` or
    10|    `clawteam harness`, so the agent automatically knows how to coordinate.
    11|    """
    12|    return f"""\
    13|## ClawTeam Runtime
    14|
    15|You are running inside ClawTeam, an agent orchestration framework.
    16|Your identity: **{agent_name}** in team **{team}**.
    17|
    18|### Available Commands
    19|- `clawteam task list {team} --owner {agent_name}` — View your assigned tasks
    20|- `clawteam task list {team}` — Fall back to the full task board if assignment has not been claimed yet
    21|- `clawteam task update {team} <id> --status in_progress` — Start working on a task
    22|- `clawteam task update {team} <id> --status completed` — Mark task as done
    23|- `clawteam inbox receive {team} --agent {agent_name}` — Check for messages
    24|- `clawteam inbox send {team} <to> "<message>"` — Message a teammate
    25|- `clawteam workspace checkpoint {team}` — Commit current progress
    26|- `clawteam lifecycle idle {team}` — Signal you're ready for more work
    27|- `clawteam cost report {team} --input-tokens <N> --output-tokens <N> --cost-cents <N>` — Report costs
    28|
    29|### Protocol
    30|1. Check your tasks: `clawteam task list {team} --owner {agent_name}`
    31|2. If that list is empty, inspect `clawteam task list {team}` and your inbox before declaring yourself idle
    32|3. For each task, update status to in_progress before starting
    33|4. Commit changes frequently with git
    34|5. Mark tasks completed when done
    35|6. Check for new messages and tasks after completing your batch
    36|7. If idle, signal with `clawteam lifecycle idle {team}`
    37|8. The harness manages your lifecycle — focus on the task at hand
    38|"""
    39|
    40|
    41|def build_wrapped_prompt(
    42|    agent_name: str,
    43|    goal: str,
    44|    team: str,
    45|) -> str:

    50|## Your Task

    53|
    54|---
    55|You are agent **{agent_name}** in team **{team}**.
    56|Use `clawteam task list {team} --owner {agent_name}` to check for assigned tasks.
    57|If that is empty, fall back to `clawteam task list {team}` and your inbox before declaring yourself idle.

     2|
     3|from __future__ import annotations
     4|
     5|import threading
     6|from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
     7|from typing import Any, Callable
     8|
     9|from clawteam.events.types import HarnessEvent
    10|
    11|# ── Event type registry (plugins can register custom event types) ─────
    12|
    13|_EVENT_TYPE_REGISTRY: dict[str, type[HarnessEvent]] = {}
    14|
    15|
    16|def register_event_type(cls: type[HarnessEvent]) -> None:
    17|    """Register a custom event type so shell hooks can reference it by name."""
    18|    _EVENT_TYPE_REGISTRY[cls.__name__] = cls
    19|
    20|
    21|def resolve_event_type(name: str) -> type[HarnessEvent] | None:
    22|    """Resolve an event class by name. Checks registry first, then types module."""
    23|    if name in _EVENT_TYPE_REGISTRY:
    24|        return _EVENT_TYPE_REGISTRY[name]
    25|    from clawteam.events import types as _types
    26|    cls = getattr(_types, name, None)
    27|    if cls is not None and isinstance(cls, type) and issubclass(cls, HarnessEvent):
    28|        return cls
    29|    return None
    30|
    31|Handler = Callable[[HarnessEvent], Any]
    32|
    33|
    34|class _Subscription:
    35|    __slots__ = ("handler", "priority")
    36|
    37|    def __init__(self, handler: Handler, priority: int):
    38|        self.handler = handler
    39|        self.priority = priority
    40|
    41|
    42|class EventBus:
    43|    """Central event bus for ClawTeam.
    44|
    45|    Handlers are called synchronously in priority order (lower = earlier).
    46|    Use ``emit_async`` for fire-and-forget notifications.
    47|    """
    48|
    49|    def __init__(self) -> None:
    50|        self._subscribers: dict[type, list[_Subscription]] = {}
    51|        self._lock = threading.RLock()
    52|        self._pool: ThreadPoolExecutor | None = None
    53|
    54|    # ── subscribe / unsubscribe ───────────────────────────────────────
    55|
    56|    def subscribe(
    57|        self,
    58|        event_type: type[HarnessEvent],
    59|        handler: Handler,
    60|        priority: int = 0,
    61|    ) -> None:
    62|        """Register *handler* for *event_type*.
    63|
    64|        Handlers with lower ``priority`` values run first.
    65|        """
    66|        with self._lock:
    67|            subs = self._subscribers.setdefault(event_type, [])
    68|            subs.append(_Subscription(handler, priority))
    69|            subs.sort(key=lambda s: s.priority)
    70|
    71|    def unsubscribe(
    72|        self,
    73|        event_type: type[HarnessEvent],
    74|        handler: Handler,
    75|    ) -> None:
    76|        """Remove a previously registered handler."""
    77|        with self._lock:
    78|            subs = self._subscribers.get(event_type)
    79|            if subs:
    80|                self._subscribers[event_type] = [
    81|                    s for s in subs if s.handler is not handler
    82|                ]
    83|
    84|    # ── emit ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    85|
    86|    def emit(self, event: HarnessEvent) -> list[Any]:
    87|        """Emit an event synchronously. Returns list of handler results.
    88|
    89|        For ``Before*`` events, handlers can set ``event.veto = True``
    90|        to signal cancellation (caller must check).
    91|        """
    92|        with self._lock:
    93|            subs = list(self._subscribers.get(type(event), []))
    94|        results: list[Any] = []
    95|        for sub in subs:
    96|            try:
    97|                result = sub.handler(event)
    98|                results.append(result)
    99|            except Exception:
   100|                pass  # handlers must not crash the bus
   101|        return results
   102|
   103|    def emit_async(self, event: HarnessEvent) -> None:
   104|        """Emit an event in a background thread (fire-and-forget)."""
   105|        if self._pool is None:
   106|            self._pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=2, thread_name_prefix="clawteam-events")
   107|        self._pool.submit(self.emit, event)
   108|
   109|    # ── introspection ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
   110|
   111|    def handler_count(self, event_type: type[HarnessEvent] | None = None) -> int:
   112|        """Return number of registered handlers, optionally filtered by type."""
   113|        with self._lock:
   114|            if event_type is not None:
   115|                return len(self._subscribers.get(event_type, []))
   116|            return sum(len(subs) for subs in self._subscribers.values())
   117|
   118|    def clear(self) -> None:
   119|        """Remove all subscribers."""
   120|        with self._lock:
   121|            self._subscribers.clear()

     2|
     3|from __future__ import annotations
     4|
     5|import json
     6|from typing import Any
     7|
     8|from clawteam.harness.contracts import SprintContract
     9|from clawteam.harness.strategies import AssignmentStrategy
    10|
    11|
    12|class RoundRobinAssigner(AssignmentStrategy):
    13|    """Default: round-robin assignment of contracts to agents."""
    14|
    15|    def assign(
    16|        self,
    17|        contracts: list,
    18|        agent_names: list[str],
    19|    ) -> dict[str, list]:
    20|        if not agent_names:
    21|            return {}
    22|        mapping: dict[str, list] = {name: [] for name in agent_names}
    23|        for i, contract in enumerate(contracts):
    24|            agent = agent_names[i % len(agent_names)]
    25|            mapping[agent].append(contract)
    26|        return mapping
    27|
    28|
    29|class ContractExecutor:
    30|    """Parses sprint contracts from artifacts and creates executable tasks."""
    31|
    32|    def __init__(
    33|        self,
    34|        orchestrator: Any,
    35|        assignment: AssignmentStrategy | None = None,
    36|    ):
    37|        self._orch = orchestrator
    38|        self._assignment = assignment or RoundRobinAssigner()
    39|
    40|    def load_contracts(self) -> list[SprintContract]:
    41|        """Load contracts from the artifact store."""
    42|        contracts: list[tuple[str, SprintContract]] = []
    43|        artifacts = self._orch.artifacts.list_artifacts()
    44|        for art in artifacts:
    45|            if "sprint-contract" in art["name"] and art["name"].endswith(".json"):
    46|                content = self._orch.artifacts.read(art["name"])
    47|                if content:
    48|                    try:
    49|                        data = json.loads(content)
    50|                        contracts.append((art["name"], SprintContract.model_validate(data)))
    51|                    except Exception:
    52|                        pass
    53|        contracts.sort(key=lambda item: (item[1].wave, item[0], item[1].id))
    54|        return [contract for _, contract in contracts]
    55|
    56|    def create_tasks_from_contracts(self, agent_names: list[str] | None = None) -> list:
    57|        """Convert contracts to TaskStore tasks with wave dependencies."""
    58|        from clawteam.team.models import TaskPriority
    59|        from clawteam.team.tasks import TaskStore
    60|
    61|        contracts = self.load_contracts()
    62|        store = TaskStore(self._orch.team_name)
    63|        created_tasks = []
    64|        wave_task_ids: dict[int, list[str]] = {}  # wave -> [task_ids]
    65|        assigned_contracts = self._assignment.assign(contracts, agent_names or [])
    66|        contract_assignees: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    67|        for agent_name, items in assigned_contracts.items():
    68|            for contract in items:
    69|                contract_assignees.setdefault(contract.id, []).append(agent_name)
    70|
    71|        for contract in contracts:
    72|            # Determine blocked_by from previous waves
    73|            blocked_by: list[str] = []
    74|            for dep_wave in range(1, contract.wave):
    75|                blocked_by.extend(wave_task_ids.get(dep_wave, []))
    76|
    77|            assignees = list(contract.assigned_to or contract_assignees.get(contract.id, []))
    78|            owner = assignees[0] if assignees else ""
    79|            if assignees:
    80|                contract.assigned_to = assignees
    81|
    82|            task = store.create(
    83|                subject=contract.title,
    84|                description=contract.description,
    85|                owner=owner,
    86|                priority=TaskPriority.high,
    87|                blocked_by=blocked_by if blocked_by else None,
    88|                metadata={
    89|                    "contract_id": contract.id,
    90|                    "wave": contract.wave,
    91|                    "assigned_to": assignees,
    92|                },
    93|            )
    94|
    95|            wave_task_ids.setdefault(contract.wave, []).append(task.id)
    96|            contract.tasks = [task.id]
    97|            created_tasks.append(task)
    98|
    99|        return created_tasks
   100|
   101|    def assign_to_agents(
   102|        self,
   103|        agent_names: list[str],
   104|    ) -> dict[str, list[SprintContract]]:
   105|        """Assign contracts to agents using the assignment strategy."""
   106|        contracts = self.load_contracts()
   107|        return self._assignment.assign(contracts, agent_names)
   108|
   109|    def check_wave_completion(self, wave: int) -> bool:
   110|        """Check if all tasks in a wave are completed."""
   111|        from clawteam.team.models import TaskStatus
   112|        from clawteam.team.tasks import TaskStore
   113|
   114|        store = TaskStore(self._orch.team_name)
   115|        tasks = store.list_tasks()
   116|        wave_tasks = [
   117|            t for t in tasks
   118|            if t.metadata.get("wave") == wave
   119|        ]
   120|        return all(t.status == TaskStatus.completed for t in wave_tasks)
