{"content": "     1|# ClawTeam Coordination Workflows
     2|
     3|## Workflow 1: Create a Team and Assign Tasks
     4|
     5|A common workflow for setting up a new project team.
     6|
     7|```bash
     8|# 1. Set leader identity
     9|export CLAWTEAM_AGENT_ID="leader-001"
    10|export CLAWTEAM_AGENT_NAME="leader"
    11|export CLAWTEAM_AGENT_TYPE="leader"
    12|
    13|# 2. Create team
    14|clawteam team spawn-team my-project -d "Web app development" -n leader
    15|
    16|# 3. Create tasks with dependencies
    17|clawteam task create my-project "Design API schema" -o leader
    18|# => Task ID: aaa11111
    19|
    20|clawteam task create my-project "Implement backend" -o backend-dev --blocked-by aaa11111
    21|# => Task ID: bbb22222 (auto-set to blocked status)
    22|
    23|clawteam task create my-project "Build frontend" -o frontend-dev --blocked-by aaa11111
    24|# => Task ID: ccc33333
    25|
    26|clawteam task create my-project "Integration testing" --blocked-by bbb22222,ccc33333
    27|# => Task ID: ddd44444
    28|
    29|# 4. Check board
    30|clawteam board show my-project
    31|
    32|# 5. As tasks complete, update status (auto-unblocks dependents)
    33|clawteam task update my-project aaa11111 --status completed
    34|# bbb22222 and ccc33333 auto-unblock from blocked -> pending
    35|```
    36|
    37|## Workflow 2: Multi-Agent Spawn and Coordination
    38|
    39|Full lifecycle of spawning multiple agents and coordinating work.
    40|
    41|```bash
    42|# Leader creates team
    43|clawteam team spawn-team dev-team -d "Feature development" -n leader
    44|
    45|# Spawn worker agents (each gets identity env vars automatically)
    46|clawteam spawn tmux claude --team dev-team --agent-name researcher --agent-type researcher
    47|clawteam spawn tmux claude --team dev-team --agent-name coder --agent-type general-purpose
    48|
    49|# Leader creates tasks
    50|clawteam task create dev-team "Research best practices" -o researcher
    51|clawteam task create dev-team "Implement solution" -o coder
    52|
    53|# Leader sends instructions via inbox
    54|clawteam inbox send dev-team researcher "Research authentication patterns for microservices"
    55|clawteam inbox send dev-team coder "Wait for researcher's findings before starting implementation"
    56|
    57|# Monitor progress
    58|clawteam board live dev-team --interval 5
    59|```
    60|
    61|### Worker Agent Perspective
    62|
    63|From inside a spawned worker agent:
    64|
    65|```bash
    66|# Identity is pre-set via environment
    67|clawteam identity show
    68|# => agentName: researcher, teamName: dev-team
    69|
    70|# Check inbox for instructions
    71|clawteam inbox receive dev-team
    72|
    73|# Do work, then update task
    74|clawteam task update dev-team <task-id> --status in_progress
    75|# ... work ...
    76|clawteam task update dev-team <task-id> --status completed
    77|
    78|# Notify leader when idle
    79|clawteam lifecycle idle dev-team --last-task <task-id> --task-status completed
    80|```
    81|
    82|## Workflow 3: Join Request Protocol
    83|
    84|When an agent wants to join an existing team dynamically.
    85|
    86|```bash
    87|# Agent side: request to join (blocks until response)
    88|clawteam team request-join dev-team bob --capabilities "frontend specialist" --timeout 120
    89|
    90|# Leader side: check inbox for join requests
    91|clawteam inbox peek dev-team --agent leader
    92|# => join_request from bob, requestId: join-abc123
    93|
    94|# Leader approves
    95|clawteam team approve-join dev-team join-abc123
    96|
    97|# Agent receives approval with assigned name and agent ID
    98|# => Approved! Joined as 'bob' (agentId: xyz789)
    99|```
   100|
   101|## Workflow 4: Plan Approval Flow
   102|
   103|For teams requiring plan review before execution.
   104|
   105|```bash
   106|# Worker submits plan
   107|clawteam plan submit dev-team coder "1. Refactor auth module\n2. Add OAuth2\n3. Update tests" \
   108|  --summary "Auth system modernization"
   109|
   110|# Leader reviews (checks inbox)
   111|clawteam inbox receive dev-team --agent leader
   112|# => plan_approval_request with planId
   113|
   114|# Leader approves or rejects
   115|clawteam plan approve dev-team <plan-id> coder --feedback "Looks good, proceed"
   116|# or
   117|clawteam plan reject dev-team <plan-id> coder --feedback "Add error handling section"
   118|```
   119|
   120|## Workflow 5: Graceful Shutdown
   121|
   122|Coordinated shutdown of team agents.
   123|
   124|```bash
   125|# Leader requests shutdown of a worker
   126|clawteam lifecycle request-shutdown dev-team leader coder --reason "All tasks complete"
   127|
   128|# Worker checks inbox, sees shutdown request
   129|clawteam inbox receive dev-team --agent coder
   130|# => shutdown_request, requestId: shut-xyz
   131|
   132|# Worker finishes current work, then approves
   133|clawteam lifecycle approve-shutdown dev-team shut-xyz coder
   134|
   135|# Leader cleans up team when all agents are done
   136|clawteam team cleanup dev-team --force
   137|```
   138|
   139|## Workflow 6: Monitoring and Debugging
   140|
   141|Using board and inbox commands to monitor team health.
   142|
   143|```bash
   144|# Quick overview of all teams
   145|clawteam board overview
   146|
   147|# Detailed view of one team
   148|clawteam board show dev-team
   149|
   150|# JSON output for scripting/parsing
   151|clawteam --json board show dev-team | jq '.taskSummary'
   152|clawteam --json task list dev-team --status blocked | jq '.[].subject'
   153|
   154|# Check who has unread messages
   155|clawteam --json board show dev-team | jq '.members[] | select(.inboxCount > 0) | .name'
   156|
   157|# Live monitoring
   158|clawteam board live dev-team --interval 3
   159|
   160|# Watch a specific agent's inbox
   161|clawteam inbox watch dev-team --agent leader
   162|```
   163|
   164|## Common Patterns
   165|
   166|### Task with Dependencies
   167|
   168|```bash
   169|# Create a chain: A -> B -> C
   170|clawteam task create team "Task A" -o alice
   171|# ID: aaa
   172|clawteam task create team "Task B" -o bob --blocked-by aaa
   173|# ID: bbb (status: blocked)
   174|clawteam task create team "Task C" -o carol --blocked-by bbb
   175|# ID: ccc (status: blocked)
   176|
   177|# When A completes, B auto-unblocks
   178|clawteam task update team aaa --status completed
   179|# B moves from blocked -> pending
   180|
   181|# When B completes, C auto-unblocks
   182|clawteam task update team bbb --status completed
   183|```
   184|
   185|### Broadcasting Updates
   186|
   187|```bash
   188|# Leader broadcasts to all team members
   189|clawteam inbox broadcast dev-team "Sprint planning at 2pm. Check your tasks."
   190|
   191|# Broadcast with routing key for filtering
   192|clawteam inbox broadcast dev-team "Build passed" --key "ci-notification"
   193|```
   194|
   195|### Using JSON Output in Scripts
   196|
   197|```bash
   198|# Get all blocked tasks
   199|BLOCKED=$(clawteam --json task list dev-team --status blocked)
   200|echo "$BLOCKED" | jq -r '.[].id'
   201|
   202|# Count pending messages per team
   203|clawteam --json board overview | jq '.[] | "\(.name): \(.pendingMessages) pending"'
   204|
   205|# Get team member names
   206|clawteam --json team status dev-team | jq -r '.members[].name'
   207|```

COMPREHENSIVE ANALYSIS OF CLAWTEAM (Roscoe-team Repository)

1. WHAT THIS SYSTEM IS

ClawTeam (v0.3.0) is a framework-agnostic multi-agent coordination CLI from HKUDS (Hong Kong University Data Science group). The CLI binary is "clawteam" (aliased "oh" in docs). Its core premise: AI agents themselves use CLI commands to self-organize into collaborative "swarms" -- no custom SDK or API integration needed.

The key insight: agents coordinate via shell commands. Any CLI-based AI agent (Claude Code, Codex, OpenClaw, nanobot, Kimi, custom scripts) can participate just by executing shell commands in its tmux pane.

2. ARCHITECTURE

The codebase is organized under clawteam/ with these major subsystems:

TEAM LAYER (clawteam/team/):
- models.py: Core Pydantic models -- TeamConfig, TeamMember, TeamMessage, TaskItem with statuses (pending, in_progress, completed, blocked) and priorities (low/medium/high/urgent). MessageType is a rich enum with 12 types: message, join_request, join_approved, plan_approval_request, shutdown_request, idle, broadcast, etc.
- manager.py: TeamManager -- CRUD for teams, member management
- router.py: RuntimeRouter -- normalizes inbox messages into RuntimeEnvelope objects, asks a routing policy for a RouteDecision, and dispatches (optionally injecting into live tmux panes)
- costs.py: CostEvent/CostSummary -- per-agent token and cost tracking
- waiter.py: TaskWaiter -- blocks until all tasks reach completed status

STORE LAYER (clawteam/store/):
- base.py: BaseTaskStore ABC with methods: create, get, update, list_tasks, release_stale_locks, get_stats
- file.py: FileTaskStore -- each task is a separate JSON file (task-{id}.json), concurrent access serialized with OS-level advisory locks (fcntl on Unix, msvcrt on Windows). Supports task locking per-agent, cycle detection in dependencies, auto-unblocking dependents on completion, and duration tracking.

TRANSPORT LAYER (clawteam/team/ + docs/transport-architecture.md):
Two backends, configured via CLAWTEAM_TRANSPORT env var:
- FileTransport (default): JSON files in inbox dirs, atomic write via tmp+rename, sorted glob for ordered reads
- P2PTransport (optional, requires pyzmq): ZeroMQ PUSH/PULL for real-time messaging, with FileTransport as automatic offline fallback. Peers discover each other via peers/*.json files containing bind addresses. PID liveness checks before attempting ZMQ delivery.

The MailboxManager sits above Transport, handling serialization of TeamMessage (Pydantic) to/from JSON bytes, then delegating I/O to the transport.

SPAWN LAYER (clawteam/spawn/):
- base.py: SpawnBackend ABC -- spawn() and list_running()
- tmux_backend.py: TmuxBackend -- THE primary backend. Creates tmux sessions named "clawteam-{team}", each agent gets a named window. Handles: env var injection via sourced temp files, workspace trust prompt auto-confirmation (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini), CLI-specific prompt injection, pane PID capture, tmux hooks for lifecycle management (pane-exited, pane-died), tiled pane view for monitoring.
- subprocess_backend.py: SubprocessBackend -- for one-shot non-interactive tools
- adapters.py: NativeCliAdapter -- normalizes commands across agent CLIs (Claude, Codex, nanobot, Gemini, Kimi, OpenCode, Qwen, Pi). Each CLI has specific flags for prompt injection, skip-permissions, non-interactive mode.
- registry.py: Agent registry for liveness checking (tmux pane PID tracking)
- profiles.py / presets.py: Reusable agent runtime profiles (model, base_url, api_key_env, etc.) and provider template presets

WORKSPACE LAYER (clawteam/workspace/):
- manager.py: WorkspaceManager -- git worktree-based isolation. Each agent gets branch "clawteam/{team}/{agent}", separate working directory. Supports create, checkpoint (auto-commit), merge back to main, cleanup.
- git.py: Git operations wrapper
- conflicts.py: Merge conflict detection and notification

BOARD LAYER (clawteam/board/):
- collector.py: Collects team state for display
- renderer.py: Rich-based terminal rendering -- kanban board, overview table, live auto-refresh
- server.py: Web UI with SSE real-time updates
- gource.py: Gource visualization integration

HARNESS LAYER (clawteam/harness/) -- THIS IS THE KEY ORCHESTRATION ENGINE:
- phases.py: Phase state machine. Default phases: DISCUSS -> PLAN -> EXECUTE -> VERIFY -> SHIP. PhaseGate ABC for gating transitions: ArtifactRequiredGate, AllTasksCompleteGate, HumanApprovalGate. PhaseRunner manages transitions, saves state to JSON.
- roles.py: Three default roles -- PLANNER (discuss/plan phases), EXECUTOR (execute phase), EVALUATOR (verify phase). Each has a system_prompt_addon.
- contracts.py: SprintContract model -- title, description, success_criteria (testable), wave number for parallel execution, depends_on.
- contract_executor.py: Converts sprint contracts to TaskStore tasks with wave-based dependencies. RoundRobin assignment strategy.
- orchestrator.py: HarnessOrchestrator -- coordinates the full harness run. Manages phase state, artifact registration, gate checking. Default gates: spec.md required before PLAN advances, all tasks complete before VERIFY advances, human approval at PLAN.
- conductor.py: HarnessConductor -- the polling loop that drives the harness. Reads exit journals, advances phases, spawns agents per phase, health checks. Uses pluggable strategies: SpawnStrategy, RespawnStrategy, HealthStrategy, ExitNotifier.
- prompts.py: Builds the auto-injected coordination prompt (the protocol every spawned agent receives).
- strategies.py: ABCs for SpawnStrategy, RespawnStrategy, HealthStrategy, ExitNotifier, AssignmentStrategy
- spawner.py: PhaseRoleSpawner -- default spawn strategy, spawns agents by phase-role mapping

EVENT BUS (clawteam/events/):
- bus.py: Synchronous pub-sub EventBus with priority ordering. Supports emit (sync) and emit_async (fire-and-forget via ThreadPoolExecutor). Events include: PhaseTransition, BeforeWorkerSpawn, AfterWorkerSpawn, BeforeTaskCreate, AfterTaskUpdate, TaskCompleted.
- Before* events support vetoing via event.veto = True

PLUGIN SYSTEM (clawteam/plugins/):
- base.py: HarnessPlugin ABC -- on_register(ctx), contribute_gates(), contribute_prompts(). Receives HarnessContext with access to bus, task store, spawner, sessions, artifacts.
- ralph_loop_plugin.py: Example plugin for re-spawning agents

MCP SERVER (clawteam/mcp/):
- server.py: FastMCP server exposing ClawTeam tools (task, inbox, workspace, cost, plan, team, board, mailbox)
- Registered as "clawteam-mcp" entry point

3. WORKFLOW MODEL

Task Flow:
pending -> in_progress -> completed (or blocked -> pending when dependency resolves)
Dependencies are expressed via --blocked-by. When a task completes, FileTaskStore._resolve_dependents_unlocked() scans all tasks and auto-unblocks any that depended on it.

Agent Collaboration:
Agents coordinate purely through CLI commands. When spawned, each agent receives an auto-injected coordination prompt teaching it the protocol:
1. Check tasks: clawteam task list {team} --owner {name}
2. Start working: clawteam task update {team} {id} --status in_progress
3. Complete: clawteam task update {team} {id} --status completed
4. Message: clawteam inbox send {team} {recipient} "message"
5. Check inbox: clawteam inbox receive {team}
6. Signal idle: clawteam lifecycle idle {team}

Scene Concept:
There is NO explicit "scene" abstraction in the code. The word "scene" appears only in asset filenames (scene-hedgefund.png, scene-engineering.png) and in the README's use case demos. The term seems to be a marketing/conceptual term for "use case template" rather than a code construct.

Harness Workflow (the structured approach):
DISCUSS -> PLAN -> EXECUTE -> VERIFY -> SHIP
- DISCUSS: Planner agent analyzes the goal, asks clarifying questions
- PLAN: Planner produces spec.md and sprint contracts. Human approval gate.
- EXECUTE: Executors spawn, contracts converted to tasks with wave-based parallelism
- VERIFY: Evaluator tests implementation against success criteria
- SHIP: Final phase, harness completes

4. TOML TEMPLATE FORMAT

Templates define team compositions. Structure:

[template]
name = "hedge-fund"
description = "AI Hedge Fund..."
command = ["claude"]          # Default CLI agent
backend = "tmux"              # tmux or subprocess
harness = true                # Optional: enable harness mode

[template.harness]            # Optional harness config
phases = ["discuss", "plan", "execute", "verify", "ship"]
human_gates = ["plan"]
wave_parallelism = 3
evaluator_count = 1

[template.leader]
name = "portfolio-manager"
type = "portfolio-manager"
task = """..."""              # {goal}, {team_name}, {agent_name} substitution

[[template.agents]]           # Array of worker agents
name = "buffett-analyst"
type = "value-analyst"
task = """..."""

[[template.tasks]]            # Pre-created tasks
subject = "Coordinate analysis"
owner = "portfolio-manager"

Built-in templates: hedge-fund (7 agents), software-dev (5 agents), code-review (4 agents), research-paper (4 agents), strategy-room (5 agents), harness-default (planner + evaluator).

5. CLI INTERFACE

The CLI is built with Typer, exposed as "clawteam" (or "oh"):

Top-level command groups:
- team: spawn-team, discover, status, cleanup, request-join, approve-join
- spawn: tmux/subprocess backends with agent-name, task, profile options
- task: create, update, list, wait (with --blocked-by, --status, --owner, --priority filters)
- inbox: send, receive, peek, broadcast, watch
- board: show, live, attach, serve, overview
- workspace: list, checkpoint, merge, cleanup
- plan: submit, approve, reject
- lifecycle: idle, request-shutdown, approve-shutdown, on-exit, on-crash
- launch: Launch a TOML template (oh launch hedge-fund --team fund1 --goal "...")
- template: list templates
- config: show, set, get, health
- profile: list, show, set, remove, test, wizard
- preset: list, show, set, set-client, copy, remove, bootstrap, generate-profile
- cost: report, summary
- harness: start, status, approve, advance, abort, conduct
- identity: show
- gource: play (visualization)

Global options: --json (JSON output), --data-dir, --transport, --version

6. COMPARISON TO GSD (IDEA->DISCUSS->RESEARCH->PLAN->APPROVE->EXECUTE->VERIFY->SHIP)

ClawTeam's harness has 5 default phases: DISCUSS -> PLAN -> EXECUTE -> VERIFY -> SHIP

Key comparisons with GSD's 8 stages:

SIMILARITIES:
- Both have a structured phase progression
- Both have gating between phases (GSD has approvals; ClawTeam has PhaseGate system)
- Both dispatch tasks to agents
- Both track task status through lifecycle states
- Both support human approval gates

DIFFERENCES:
- GSD has IDEA and RESEARCH as explicit phases; ClawTeam merges these into DISCUSS
- GSD has APPROVE as a dedicated phase; ClawTeam uses HumanApprovalGate that can be attached to any phase
- ClawTeam's phases are OPEN STRINGS (not enums) -- plugins/templates can define custom phases
- ClawTeam has SprintContract as the unit of work -- testable success criteria, wave-based parallelism
- ClawTeam's harness is optional -- the basic team/task/inbox flow works without it
- ClawTeam's agents are self-organizing via CLI commands; GSD dispatches tasks to agents from the orchestrator
- ClawTeam has two parallel models: (1) simple team/task/inbox for agent self-organization, and (2) structured harness with phases/contracts/evaluation
- ClawTeam separates the "agent spawn" concern from "task management" -- agents live in tmux, tasks live in JSON files
- GSD is more prescriptive; ClawTeam is more flexible but less opinionated about the workflow

KEY ARCHITECTURAL DIFFERENCE:
GSD's orchestrator IS the intelligence -- it decides what to do at each stage.
ClawTeam's orchestrator is infrastructure -- the AI agents ARE the intelligence, using CLI tools to self-organize. The harness provides guardrails (gates, phases, contracts) but the agents drive the work.

7. OPENCLAW INTEGRATION

OpenClaw is listed as a fully supported CLI agent alongside Claude Code and Codex. The integration is:
- oh spawn tmux openclaw --team ... (spawn command)
- scripts/openclaw_worker.sh: A wrapper script for non-interactive OpenClaw worker agents. It loads .env files, inherits model config from parent agent, runs "openclaw agent --local" with the task, captures output, and sends results back via "clawteam inbox send".
- The spawn/adapters.py normalizes OpenClaw commands (no special adapter class visible, it appears treated as a generic CLI)

This is NOT a fork of OpenClaw. ClawTeam is a coordination framework that can USE OpenClaw as one of many agent backends. It wraps OpenClaw the same way it wraps Claude Code or Codex.

8. TRANSPORT LAYER

Two transport backends, selectable via CLAWTEAM_TRANSPORT:

FileTransport (default):
- Messages stored as JSON files in teams/{team}/inboxes/{agent}/msg-{timestamp}.json
- Atomic writes: write to .tmp-xxx.json, then os.rename
- Reads: sorted glob of msg-*.json, read+unlink for consume, read-only for peek
- Zero dependencies beyond filesystem

P2PTransport (optional, requires pyzmq):
- ZeroMQ PUSH/PULL sockets for real-time delivery
- Each agent binds a PULL socket on startup, writes address to peers/{agent}.json
- Sender reads peer address, checks PID liveness, connects PUSH socket and sends
- If peer is offline (no address file, or PID dead), falls back to FileTransport
- On receive: drain ZMQ socket first (NOBLOCK), then check file fallback
- Messages are transient (ZMQ) with persistent fallback (files)

The two transport layers are completely orthogonal to the shared filesystem used for team config, task state, and plan storage. P2P only handles the inbox messaging channel.

SUMMARY OF FINDINGS:
- ClawTeam is a 6,800+ line Python framework for multi-agent coordination
- CLI-first design: agents use shell commands, not APIs
- Filesystem-backed state (JSON files) with no database dependencies
- tmux-based agent isolation with git worktrees for code isolation
- Pluggable: transport, store, plugins, custom phases, custom templates
- The harness provides a structured DISCUSS->PLAN->EXECUTE->VERIFY->SHIP pipeline with sprint contracts
- OpenClaw is supported as a backend agent, not forked
- Rich monitoring: terminal kanban, live dashboard, web UI, tiled tmux view
